title: v0.9 /debug Slash Command Log-Leak Warning
summary: v0.9 era: the `/debug` slash command uploaded sensitive logs to a public site with no confirmation prompt. Affects WhatsApp usernames, memory entries with personal info. Status in v0.13/v0.14 unverified — audit before use. Source 1skl8le (259/52).

slash_commands.purpose: v0.9 era: uploaded sensitive logs to public site without confirmation — verify behavior in current version before invoking

gotchas: u/TheLadDothCallMe (+5): 'The /debug slash command just uploads a bunch of sensitive logs with no confirmation to a public site. WhatsApp usernames, memories containing personal information.' v0.9 confirmed; later versions unverified — DO NOT run /debug without auditing destination + payload first
full_text:
Source: r/hermesagent post 1skl8le on v0.9.0 release thread (259 score, 52 comments).

u/TheLadDothCallMe (+5) reported the /debug slash command uploaded sensitive logs to a public site with no confirmation prompt — WhatsApp usernames, memories containing personal info. v0.9 era. Status in v0.13/v0.14 not verified.

## Recommended check before /debug
- What endpoint does /debug upload to?
- Is the upload behind an explicit y/N prompt?
- Does the payload include memory entries or messaging-platform metadata?

If any of these are unclear, do not run /debug in a production session.
